_meta.purpose: Cited snapshot of what GvProf, DrGPUM, and GPA are publicly known to cover, used by scripts/check_coverage.py to answer 'is this candidate benchmark already covered?' without a live web search per candidate. This is a snapshot, not a live source of truth -- refresh it (and bump lastChecked) if it's more than a few months old before trusting a 'not-found' result.
_meta.howGathered: GitHub repo contents/.gitmodules inspected directly via `gh api` for each tool's actual public repo -- not inferred from memory or general knowledge.

gvprof.paper: K. Zhou et al., 'GVPROF: A Value Profiler for GPU-Based Clusters', SC20 -- https://mxz297.github.io/files/SC20.pdf
gvprof.note: GVProf's .gitmodules points its 'samples' submodule at this shared repo -- same one DrGPUM uses.

drgpum.paper: DrGPUM: Guiding Memory Optimization for GPU-Accelerated Applications, ASPLOS 2023 -- https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3582016.3582044
drgpum.note: DrGPUM's .gitmodules points its 'samples' submodule at the identical shared repo GVProf uses -- for benchmark-coverage purposes, GVProf and DrGPUM should be treated as covering the same suite, not checked as if independent.

papers: K. Zhou et al., 'GPA: A GPU Performance Advisor Based on Instruction Sampling', CGO 2021
K. Zhou et al., 'An Automated Tool for Analysis and Tuning of GPU-accelerated Code in HPC Applications', TPDS 2021
gpa.note: GPA's own README example (`./bin/bench.sh rodinia/bfs`) and GPA-Benchmark's contents confirm its suite is the rodinia benchmark set (plus per-benchmark '-opt' reference variants), not the GVProf/DrGPUM shared samples repo.

crossToolObservations: GvProf and DrGPUM share the exact same benchmark suite submodule (FindHao/hpctoolkit-gpu-sanitizer-samples) -- a candidate 'covered by GvProf but not DrGPUM' (or vice versa) is not possible under this snapshot; treat them as one combined coverage set.
kmeans is covered by GPA but absent from the GvProf/DrGPUM shared suite.
dwt2d, hotspot3D, and nn appear in the GvProf/DrGPUM shared suite but are absent from GPA-Benchmark's rodinia set.

leukocyte, mummergpu, and hybridsort appear in neither list checked here -- candidates worth checking further if they surface in a paper survey, though their absence from these two suites is not proof they're uncovered by every profiling tool that exists, only by these three.
